data.title: end-session: prior session abandoned merge-to-main and pushed to remote worktree branch instead of resolving staged-file conflict
data.description:
## Incident Overview
A prior session working on epic f06e-56d3 (Code Review Decision Gate) completed all implementation work (12 commits) but failed to merge to main. Instead of diagnosing and resolving the failure, the session pushed the worktree branch to the remote and exited, deferring the merge to the next session.

## Evidence at Session Start (2026-04-07)
- Worktree branch `worktree-20260407-091501` was 12 commits ahead of main, 0 behind (clean fast-forward candidate)
- `git status` in the worktree was clean
- Main repo (`/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra`) had **82 staged files** in the index — test files and agent files from a prior session that were staged but never committed
- `merge-to-main.sh` failed at `_phase_merge` with: `error: Your local changes to the following files would be overwritten by merge`
- The script's `_cleanup_stale_git_state` did not detect the staged changes (separate bug: 96c1-a4a6)
- Stash list contained 8 entries from various prior sessions, including `stash@{0}: WIP on feature: 704a3d3c` from another branch

## Root Cause
The prior session encountered the merge failure and lacked the diagnostic persistence to identify that the main repo's staged index was the blocker. The fix was straightforward: `git reset HEAD && git checkout . && git clean -fd` in the main repo, then retry. Instead, the session took the path of least resistance — pushing the branch to remote and leaving the merge for the next operator.

## Impact
- User had to spend a follow-up session solely on merge cleanup
- Worktree branch sat unmerged on remote, creating drift risk
- 82 orphaned staged files accumulated in main repo working tree

## Expected Behavior
/dso:end-session (or the agent running it) should diagnose merge-to-main failures rather than abandoning the merge. When `_phase_merge` fails, the agent should:
1. Check `git status` in the main repo to identify the blocker
2. If staged/dirty files are the cause, propose resetting them (with user confirmation)
3. Only push to remote as a last resort after exhausting resolution attempts

## Fix Direction
This is an LLM-behavioral issue. The end-session skill could add explicit guidance: when merge-to-main.sh fails, check main repo state (`git -C MAIN_REPO status --short`) before giving up. Consider adding a diagnostic step to the skill between merge failure and the current error-relay-and-stop behavior.
